notes: Divergence records are kept separate from active intent records. They preserve implementation-vs-target mismatches for review without polluting the primary target contract map.

records.title: Child Finer-Scale Model Request Path Conflicts With Active+Above Policy
records.summary: Current flow/runtime signals include child/finer-scale model request paths, which conflicts with the locked no-below-active simulation rule.
records.description: Implementation-facing docs and modules still reflect detail-scale expansion mechanics below active scale, while owner direction locks simulation scope to active scale plus coarser scales only.
records.current_implementation_observation: Observed in flow docs and phenomenon runtime modules.
records.owner_direction: No exceptions: scales below the currently active scale are never actively or fully simulated.

sources.ref: conversation: no exceptions, scales below active are never actively/fully simulated

implementation_semantics.reason: Current docs/code indicate the path exists, but exact runtime behavior details are still under redesign.

current_implementation_observation.reason: Owner direction treats current USF code/docs as non-authoritative clean-slate signals.

records.title: Bootstrap Descent Controller Implementation Is Still Zone-Era And Under-Scoped
records.summary: Current bootstrap/worldgen implementation shape is aligned in direction but materially under-scoped and still tied to zone-era readiness assumptions.
records.description: Owner direction keeps the bootstrap descent controller concept but requires a zone-free, explicit Genesis/Evolve contract with scale progression, stabilization gates, and timeline catch-up semantics.
records.current_implementation_observation: Observed in worldgen module and flow docs.
records.owner_direction: Retain bootstrap descent controller concept, but elevate it to a formal Genesis/Evolve contract decoupled from zone-era assumptions.

sources.ref: conversation: aligned in direction but only partially there; move implementation-shaped part to divergences; rewrite as target Genesis/Evolve contract

implementation_semantics.reason: Existing bootstrap behavior is visible, but future contract semantics are still being formalized.

current_implementation_observation.reason: Owner direction treats current USF code/docs as non-authoritative clean-slate signals.

records.title: Temporal Context Implementation Is Zone-Coupled But Target Is Scale-Native
records.summary: Current temporal context plumbing depends on zone resources, while owner direction requires zone-free scale-native temporal semantics.
records.description: Implementation currently computes scale factors from zone-coupled resources. Target design requires temporal context anchored directly in scale primitives and aligned with active+above simulation policy.
records.current_implementation_observation: Observed in zone temporal context resource code.
records.owner_direction: Temporal context must be rewritten as a zone-free scale-time model.

sources.ref: conversation: zones removed; rewrite temporal context to zone-free scale-native contract

implementation_semantics.reason: Current scale-factor behavior is visible, but migration semantics to scale-native temporal model remain open.

current_implementation_observation.reason: Owner direction treats current USF code/docs as non-authoritative clean-slate signals.

records.title: Canonical Docs Remain Zone-Centric While Target Model Removes Zones
records.summary: Canonical architecture/flow/prototype docs still encode zones as major USF concepts, conflicting with the locked remove-zones target direction.
records.description: Current canonical docs preserve a zone-centric model, but owner direction treats zones as fully deprecated and replaced by per-scale Metrics + PhenomenaRealizer + Phenomena.
records.owner_direction: Remove zones entirely from target USF vocabulary and runtime contracts.

sources.ref: conversation: remove zones entirely; only Metrics + PhenomenaRealizer + Phenomena per scale

migration_scope.reason: Full doc migration plan and sequencing are still open.

current_implementation_observation.reason: Owner direction treats current USF docs/code as non-authoritative clean-slate signals.

records.title: Prototype Determinism Wording Conflicts With Statistical-Consistency Direction
records.summary: Prototype acceptance text still implies deterministic equal-seed behavior, while owner direction defines macro-law consistency with stochastic drift.
records.description: Determinism framing in prototype docs is out of sync with target semantics for seed/content/timeline behavior.
records.owner_direction: Use statistical consistency under shared macro laws, with expected timeline drift.

sources.ref: conversation: statistically consistent universe with expected drift; same macro laws

semantic_precision.reason: Formal acceptance criteria for macro-law consistency and drift are still being specified.

current_implementation_observation.reason: Owner direction treats current USF docs/code as non-authoritative clean-slate signals.

records.title: Legacy 2D Scale-Depth Layering Conflicts With 3D Multi-Scale Composition Direction
records.summary: Current scale module hard-codes canonical render depth layering per scale, a leftover 2D policy that does not match current 3D composition direction.
records.description: Owner direction requires richer multi-scale composition with explicit logic-vs-representation separation and recursive phenomenon subregioning. Fixed scale->depth mapping is treated as legacy implementation policy.
records.current_implementation_observation: Observed in scale render-depth constants and helpers.
records.owner_direction: Deprecate this 2D-era depth-layer policy in favor of 3D composition contracts.

sources.ref: conversation: this is a leftover from 2D; 3D needs recursive subregions and explicit frontend/backend entity split

replacement_contract.reason: Legacy mismatch is clear, but full replacement render/composition contract is still being designed.

current_implementation_observation.reason: Owner direction treats current USF code/docs as non-authoritative clean-slate signals.

records.title: Canonical Demo Content Remains Zone-Centric And Misaligned With Target USF Model
records.summary: Current runnable demo scripts still encode zone-centric concepts and behaviors, conflicting with the locked remove-zones direction.
records.description: Executable demo content is acknowledged as severely misaligned with target USF semantics and should be treated as legacy migration context, not as quality or architecture reference.
records.current_implementation_observation: Observed in active script tree content.
records.owner_direction: Remove zones entirely and avoid treating current demo content quality/structure as target direction.

sources.ref: conversation: current demo content is implemented terribly overall

migration_path.reason: Misalignment is clear, but exact replacement sequencing for demo content is still open.

current_implementation_observation.reason: Owner direction treats current USF code/docs as non-authoritative clean-slate signals.

records.title: Script Ownership Boundary Diverges From Gameplay-Side Target Placement
records.summary: Docs signal that most scripts should eventually live gameplay-side, but current primary USF scripts remain under engine-side assets.
records.description: This is a packaging/ownership divergence rather than a direct USF semantics contract, but it impacts future content architecture and migration planning.
records.current_implementation_observation: Observed in README notes and current script tree placement.
records.owner_direction: Treat as divergence to be resolved through content-boundary migration decisions.

sources.ref: conversation: this should be treated as divergence

migration_timing.reason: Target boundary direction exists, but sequencing/timing remains open.

current_implementation_observation.reason: Owner direction treats current USF code/docs as non-authoritative clean-slate signals.

records.title: Capability-Channel Implementation Depth Diverges From Script-First Operational Target
records.summary: Target direction relies on script-first capability channels, but current implementation depth/adoption is still minimal and far from target operational shape.
records.description: Owner indicates runtime behavior is overwhelmingly direct Bevy/Rust capability usage, with capability-channel architecture barely implemented so far. This is a direct mismatch with target script-authored orchestration.
records.current_implementation_observation: Observed from owner review and partial channel-family signals in code/docs.
records.owner_direction: Prioritize capability-channel architecture as primary operational path; treat current state as early/insufficient.

sources.ref: conversation: basically nothing uses capability channels; ~99.999% is direct native rust/bevy capability usage

implementation_measurement.reason: Gap severity is clear directionally, but no formal quantitative maturity metric is defined.

current_implementation_observation.reason: Owner direction treats current USF code/docs as non-authoritative clean-slate signals.
